title: Add system test with ginkgo
body:
The system test write with ginkgo. Most of the functions are from the e2e test. Update the default way to run podman command to fit the request of system test. The tests can be filter by --focus and --skip to fit different test target.
We also be able to set global options and cmd options by export it to ENV to fit different test matrix.

| Varible Name              | Useage  |
| :------------------------ | :-------------------------------------------------------- |
| PODMAN\_BINARY | Used to set podman binary path. Can be used for test installed rpm |
| PODMAN\_$SUBCMD\_OPTIONS | Command line options for each podman command. $SUBCMD is the short command from "podman -h". | 
| $GLOBALOPTIONS | Global options from "podman -h". The Varible Name is the option name which replace "-" with "\_" and with upper case |


***CIRRUS: SYSTEM TEST***

comments.body: @cevich PTAL
@rhatdan Thanks a lot for your comments. Update those two functions with separated patches. Will update it to e2e test later.

Haven't dived in yet, but I'm confused as to how the system tests will differ from the e2e tests.  I'm all for testing, but am concerned about how long all the tests will take.  Can we do the tests that you're concerned about in e2e, or  will the system tests not run during a PR review process?

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat 
Just as @cevich said. The system test should be used for test the installed binary in the user environment. So its will failed in current CI as the binary is not installed. I agree with Chris that we should not run this test for every pullreq. Will use it to test the rpms later.

Currently the e2e test is used as integration test, but the test cases inside it is more like the function tests. So sometimes it is confused to me too about the different between current e2e test set and system test. Maybe we should do a clean up later. 

Will summarize the common functions and Podman sesstion structure to a separated package to ignore the duplicate code. Integration test and system test only need different test structure to handle the default way to call podman and setup the options for it.

Thanks @ypu.  I appreciate the explanation of the end result.  That sounds like a fine plan to me.  Working up the code to decrease the duplication between the integration and system tests sounds very good to me.

> Thanks @ypu. I appreciate the explanation of the end result. That sounds like a fine plan to me. Working up the code to decrease the duplication between the integration and system tests sounds very good to me.

Yes, thanks @ypu.  I would really like to see unittests implemented for all the functions.  That's very important to keep the system tests working, since they are not executed as part of PR-testing.

---

Speaking of which...one option is, have Travis or ``$TEST_SYSTEM`` conditionally/optionally execute system-tests for a PR.  This could be determined by a number of ways, some examples:
* A "magic" word/phrase in a PR description or comment
* Detecting changed system-test related files in ``git log`` output.
* Some other way?

Hi @cevich,
Thanks a lot for all your comments for the functions. But as I plan to make a common utils package for integration test and system test. To ignore I missing any update to e2e package, I prefer to not change them here. But I will record them, and work on them one by one later. 

> Overall this is a good start, however I have some major concerns. Specifically, the lack of unit-tests, and a large number of library functions which aren't actually used by any test(?).

The library functions is actually one reason I recommend to use ginkgo for our test. We already have them for e2e test and we can reuse this for the system test. So I don't remove them when I copy them to system test, it may not be used by any other tests, but it help us to add tests quick. 

The library functions are inside the xxx_test.go in the past. So seems it is hard to write unit test for it. But as I planed to move them to separated lib package. This should be possible.

>Also, there is no facility I can see (Makefile?) or documentation that describes how the tests should be executed, and what are the prerequisites/initial expected state.

Still not update the Makefile for the review version. Will add it. And there is a README.md in the top level of test dir. You can get the instructions there.

> Speaking of which...one option is, have Travis or $TEST_SYSTEM conditionally/optionally execute system-tests for a PR. This could be determined by a number of ways, some examples:

As this product is not provide any binaries directly, maybe we should not run the system test for any PR?

> The library functions are inside the xxx_test.go in the past. So seems it is hard to write unit test for it. But as I planed to move them to separated lib package. This should be possible.

I can't stress how important this is.  Without unit-tests against the system-test library functions, maintenance will be a huge hassle.  Lint-checking would be nice as well, but not nearly as important.

> And there is a README.md in the top level of test dir.

Ahh, I missed that, thanks.  Since you're moving library stuff around, it would be nice to also update the docs, after you get the Makefile things squared away.

> As this product is not provide any binaries directly, maybe we should not run the system test for any PR?

The PR testing does produce binaries.  My thinking (for future work) is we make the system-tests **not** run by default against PRs, but it should be supported to run them.  This will provide a faster feedback-loop for anyone actually working on the system-tests, since waiting for the post-packaging tests is much slower.  This is just my idea, no changes needed here to support it.

Hi @cevich Sorry for the delay. Now I have finished utils package and the unittest for it. Still left some docs and Makefile update.  Will finish all the docs and cleanup work, and update the pullreq tomorrow.

Hi @cevich 
Updated the pullreq with common utils and its uint test. But put it in a separated item in Makefile. The unit test for test library will not be run for the CI. Can you help to review it? Thanks a lot.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 60e8e4f) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@ypu gosh I'm sorry.  Somehow I lost or did not get notification of your comment above.  I will take a look at your updates later today or tomorrow.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1a59c4d) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Thanks @cevich for your understanding. Will update the functions later in other PR.
Updated the README based on your comments, and put the test utils unit-test into localunit. Also fixed one bug in no-new-privileges test to make the CI passed.

Excellent!  For an initial go at this, it LGTM.   @edsantiago would you mind focusing your expert typo-spotting laser-eyes on the code here?  I've given it all a once-over, but your vision is much more sharp than mine :smile:
Thanks @edsantiago . Pullreq updated.

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat and @rhatdan 
Can you help to review this pullreq?
@baude You might want to make a pass over this too, it hits some of the E2E test code.
a few things here and there to clean up, otherwise lgtm
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1528) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@ypu had this idea #1616 maybe we can merge that, and it will help you finish this (since package-built-time testing will be slow feedback).

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat 
Already update the debug info and other comments in the docs based on your and @baude 's advice. As this pullreq is very large and need to be updated if there is any change under test. So can we just kepp this pullreq to implement the init of system test and test/utils? And we can record other request during the code review, and improve the code in later pullreqs.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1627) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@ypu, sorry I somehow missed this the other day.  I'm fine with moving forward with this PR as is, however at the moment it needs to be rebased.  Why don't you do that, see where it stands after that and we'll move forward with this if the others agree.

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat 
Thanks for understanding. And I updated the pullreq.
Already talked this with Chris as he has lots of comments about improve the test suits in this pullreqs. And seems he is OK for split the task. And will wait for others opinion.

@ypu please rebase against latest master.  That will allow the Cirrus CI ``full_vm_testing``to pass.  Alternatively, wait for...

@mheon @baude If we can get #1616 merged, that will allow _optionally_ running the system tests, as part of PR testing.  This would be handy for this PR, since @ypu would then be able to execute system testing inside the CI environment.

Hi @cevich 
The full_vm_testing is failed with "Failed to start: The resource 'projects/libpod-218412/global/images/ubuntu-1804-bionic-v20180911-libpod-fce09afe' was not found". I am afraid rebase should not fix this.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1740) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@ypu there was a change to ``.cirrus.yml`` to use ``ubuntu-1804-bionic-v20180911-libpod-63a86a18``.  That's what you need to pick up by rebase.
@cevich Thanks for your explain. Pullreq updated.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1751) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@ypu if you rebase (again) you'll [pick up a change](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1616) that optionally executes your system tests, through the Cirrus-CI system.  [The script that makes this happen](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/optional_system_test.sh), only triggers if the PR subject contains the magic string: ``***CIRRUS: SYSTEM TEST***``  Please ping me if you have any questions / problems with this.  Hopefully it will allow development in this PR to be easier.

Hi @cevich  Want confirm with you about how to trigger the system test. As you said we should include the key words in PR subject or description, but the cirrus docs marks that CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE is the git commit. Seems it should be the last commit message in the PR?

@ypu Thanks for double-checking, it seems the cirrus documentation is incorrect.  In practice, both the summary line (what you changed) ***and*** the description (big text box at the top) are encoded within `$CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE`.   I will double-confirm this, but it is what I observed in the past.  I also see that you are not on the approved-list for automatically running jobs in cirrus, I will look into fixing that.
(moved magic string into description)
(fixed cirrus-ci settings, so jobs will run automatically w/o approval for you)

@ypu Yeah, the docs are wrong.  The ``show_env_vars`` function I call shows:

```
export CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE=Add system test with ginkgo
The system test write with ginkgo. Most of the functions are from the e2e test. Update the default way to run podman command to fit the request of system test. The tests can be filter by --focus and --skip to fit different test target.
We also be able to set global options and cmd options by export it to ENV to fit different test matrix.
| Varible Name              | Useage  |
| :------------------------ | :-------------------------------------------------------- |
| PODMAN\_BINARY | Used to set podman binary path. Can be used for test installed rpm |
| PODMAN\_\_OPTIONS | Command line options for each podman command.  is the short command from "podman -h". | 
|  | Global options from "podman -h". The Varible Name is the option name which replace "-" with "\_" and with upper case |
```

That's interesting, since the run was triggered before you update the summary.  So it's time-sensitive as well.
@ypu Double Uggg...there's a bug in cirrus affecting this: https://github.com/cirruslabs/cirrus-ci-docs/issues/92
@cevich Thanks a lot for the explain. Seems the bug is fixed. Maybe we can try it with retest this pullreq.

@ypu You have access now to press the retest button in cirrus :smile:   Sadly I found [another bug in cirrus,  relating to Java regular-expression processing.](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1780#issuecomment-437349414)

***Note***: There is no harm in temporarily abusing the CI system.  e.g. you can edit the scripts to do what you like and put in a temporary commit.  Main/entry-point configuration is ``.cirrus.yml``, documentation is under ``contrib/cirrus/readme.md``.  It's very simple.

Hi @cevich 
I really don't like the idea to close this pullreq. But I understand that this may block our work for system test. 
Hi @rhatdan and @TomSweeneyRedHat Can you help check if we can get this pullreq approved? Already fixed the problems in the comments, just left some improvement request to separated tasks later. They are:
1. Set Timeout for handle different operations
2. Improve WaitForContainer.
3. Merge and update similar functions exist.
4. Reuse the sub commands in test.
If we are good to merge this pullreq first. I will file issues for them with more details to make sure we didn't miss anything for the test suite.
@cevich @edsantiago an problems with merging this PR in?
LGTM, but we need a bunch of other head nods.

LGTM
I want @cevich and @edsantiago To say it  works for them, then I will merge.

/lgtm

@lsm5 Okay, this is even easier than I thought, and it works.  Can you make a podman sub-package for RHEL and Fedora for ``system.test`` binary?  Not sure where a good place for it is, maybe `/usr/share/tests/podman/`?   Let me know if you need a jira card or any other secretarial duties I can help with :smile:
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/lgtm
I'm confused; how is `system.test` generated? I see no Makefile rule for it.

> I'm confused; how is system.test generated? I see no Makefile rule for it.

``go test ...`` produces it.  Hmm, I see `localsystem` target accidentally works because we hit `install.tools` for all the other tests.  That's def. a prerequisite (and having `$GOPATH/bin` in your $PATH).  @edsantiago @ypu maybe `Makefile` should be:

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 8860f8ab..3626a715 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -178,7 +178,7 @@ ginkgo:
 
 localintegration: varlink_generate test-binaries clientintegration ginkgo
 
-localsystem:
+localsystem: .install.ginkgo .install.gomega
        ginkgo -v -noColor test/system/
 
 clientintegration:
```

That works for me (just tried it)

...actually.  That doesn't produce the binary, only runs the tests.  How about:

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 8860f8ab..36a5adb0 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -178,9 +178,12 @@ ginkgo:
 
 localintegration: varlink_generate test-binaries clientintegration ginkgo
 
-localsystem:
+localsystem: .install.ginkgo .install.gomega
        ginkgo -v -noColor test/system/
 
+system.test: .install.ginkgo .install.gomega
+       go test -c ./test/system
+
 clientintegration:
        $(MAKE) -C contrib/python/podman integration
        $(MAKE) -C contrib/python/pypodman integration
```
Thanks @cevich and @edsantiago for the advice. Never thought we should compile system.test here, just thought it should be a part of package build. Update the Makefile now.
